heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 95–164, title III, § 306(a)91 Stat. 1322Nov. 9, 1977
label: 721 to 740 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 95–164, title III, § 306(a)91 Stat. 1322Nov. 9, 1977
notes: This chapter, covering the operation of only metal and nonmetallic mines, is covered by section 801 et seq. of this title following the enactment of which brought the operation of all coal and other mines under a single legislative canopy.Pub. L. 95–164
Section 721, , , , defined “commerce”, “mine”, “operator”, “Secretary”, and “Board”. See .Pub. L. 89–577, § 280 Stat. 772section 802 of this titleSept. 16, 1966

Section 722, , , , described mines to be covered and empowered Secretary of the Interior to decline jurisdiction if effect of the mine on commerce was not sufficiently substantial. See section 801 et seq. of this title.Pub. L. 89–577, § 380 Stat. 773Sept. 16, 1966
